_note: Expiry date formats observed on real Indian medicine packaging. Regex patterns are for use in OCR post-processing. All formats sourced from documented Indian pharmacopoeial labeling requirements and publicly visible medicine packaging.

expiry_patterns.description: Month/Year (numeric, 2-digit year) — most common format on Indian strips
expiry_patterns.parse_logic: Group 1 = month (01-12), Group 2 = year (2-digit or 4-digit). If 2-digit, prepend 20.
Group 1 = 3-letter month abbreviation, Group 2 = 4-digit year. Map abbreviation to month number.

expiry_patterns.description: 'Use before' phrasing with month abbreviation + year
expiry_patterns.parse_logic: Same month/year extraction as EXP002 but with 'USE BEFORE' prefix.

expiry_patterns.description: Full phrase 'EXPIRY DATE' followed by MM/YYYY
Manufacture date and expiry date on same line
expiry_patterns.parse_logic: Groups 1-2 = MFD month/year; Groups 3-4 = EXP month/year. Validate EXP > MFD.

expiry_patterns.description: ISO-like year-month format seen on some bulk packaging
Hindi Devanagari expiry label — 'Samapti' means expiry
expiry_patterns.parse_logic: Same month/year extraction. Requires Unicode-capable OCR.
expiry_patterns._note: Requires Devanagari-capable OCR (Tesseract with Hindi training data or Google Vision)

_ocr_implementation_notes.recommended_ocr: Google Vision API (best accuracy for Indian scripts) or Tesseract 4.x with eng+hin traineddata
_ocr_implementation_notes.post_processing: After OCR, apply regex patterns in priority order EXP001 → EXP002 → EXP003 → EXP004 → EXP005
_ocr_implementation_notes.date_validation: Reject expiry dates more than 10 years in future or already past
